codeReview.summary: Implementation correctly extracts typed nodes (File, Function, Struct/Type, Method, Import) from Rust source via tree-sitter and creates correct edge types (Contains, Calls, Imports). The extraction pipeline is well-tested with 8 unit tests covering node extraction, edge creation, type mapping, and integration. The swell-core treesitter.rs modification to handle 'use_declaration' nodes is correct.

issues.description: The 'chunk_to_node' field is populated but never read through any public method. While technically correct (clippy passes with #[expect(dead_code)]), this field adds confusion about its purpose. Consider either exposing a getter method or removing the field if truly unnecessary.

The 'insert_into_graph' async function is declared and exported but has no test coverage. The test_integration_with_knowledge_graph test validates the extraction pipeline but doesn't persist to an actual SqliteKnowledgeGraph. This is acceptable since the function is straightforward (just iterates and inserts), but a minimal test would improve confidence.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The rust-worker skill file was not found at the expected path (~/.factory/skills/rust-worker/SKILL.md). Unable to verify if worker followed the correct procedure. The transcript shows the worker followed standard crate-scoped validation patterns from AGENTS.md.

summary: Feature implementation passes review. Tree-sitter AST extraction pipeline is correctly implemented with proper node type mapping (Function, Method, Class, Module, Type for Struct/Enum/Trait, Import) and edge types (Contains, Calls, Imports, DependsOn). 8 unit tests cover extraction logic. All verification steps passed (397 swell-memory tests, 8 tree-sitter tests, clippy). Minor observations: (1) insert_into_graph lacks direct test but extraction is well-validated, (2) chunk_to_node field is unused through public API but correctly annotated. The discovered limitation about Calls edges always originating from File node (due to source_chunk not being populated in tree-sitter collection) is accurately documented in the handoff.
